subject: Human Resources
title: Non-Compete Agreements
description: A micro-lesson that makes Non-Compete Agreements usable.

hook.text: A sandwich maker at Jimmy John's had to sign a non-compete. A sandwich maker. The absurdity sparked a national debate.

buildup.text: Non-competes restrict employees from working for competitors or starting competing businesses for a set period after leaving.

discovery.text: About 18% of American workers are bound by non-competes—many in low-wage roles where trade secrets aren't a factor.

twist.text: The FTC proposed a nationwide ban in 2023, calling non-competes unfair and harmful to worker mobility and wages.

climax.text: California has banned non-competes for decades—and has the most dynamic labor market and innovation ecosystem in the country.

punchline.text: Protect trade secrets. Don't trap people in jobs they want to leave.

quiz.question: Why was the FTC's proposed ban on non-competes significant?
options: It called non-competes unfair and harmful to worker mobility
It only applied to tech workers
